description:
A former teammate left behind some helper scripts in the directory 
  /home/user/legacy_tools/.  
  One of them is an old-style Bourne-shell utility called word_count.sh.

  Your goal: execute this script on the included example text file and save the
  numeric result to a log file for later reference.

  Precise requirements the automated checker will enforce:

  1. Script to run  
     • Full path:  /home/user/legacy_tools/word_count.sh  
     • The script accepts exactly one positional argument: the full path to a
       text file.  
     • It prints a single positive integer to standard output – the number of
       whitespace-separated words in that file – and then exits with status 0.

  2. Input file  
     • Full path:  /home/user/legacy_tools/sample.txt  
     • Do not modify the file; just pass its path to the script.

  3. Output file you must create  
     • Directory:   /home/user/output/        (create it if missing)  
     • File name:   sample_wc.log  
     • Full path:   /home/user/output/sample_wc.log  
     • Contents:    The file must contain exactly one line, consisting solely
       of the integer emitted by word_count.sh when it is given
       /home/user/legacy_tools/sample.txt as its argument.  There must be
       no extra spaces, newlines, or other characters before or after the
       integer.  (The checker will treat a trailing newline as an error.)

  4. What counts as success  
     The automated test will:
       a) Confirm that /home/user/output/sample_wc.log exists.  
       b) Read the entire contents of the file; it must be the single ASCII
          number described above and nothing more.  
       c) Verify that this number is correct for the current contents of
          sample.txt.  

  In short: run the old script against the provided sample file and capture
  its sole numeric output in an identically-formatted log file located at
  /home/user/output/sample_wc.log.  A single terminal command is enough, but
  use whatever steps you prefer.  Do not print anything extra to the log file;
  keep console chatter separate from the file you create.
truth:
Pre-existing material (already on disk before the agent starts):

  1. /home/user/legacy_tools/word_count.sh  
     #!/bin/sh  
     # Simple POSIX shell script – counts words in a file sent as $1  
     wc -w "$1" | awk '{print $1}'

  2. /home/user/legacy_tools/sample.txt  
     Hello world from legacy code base.

  Because the sample text contains six space-separated words, running
    /home/user/legacy_tools/word_count.sh /home/user/legacy_tools/sample.txt
  prints exactly:
    6

  Therefore the verifier expects the file /home/user/output/sample_wc.log to
  exist and to contain the single ASCII character sequence:
    6
  with no trailing newline (file size = 1 byte).  Any deviation (extra
  whitespace, newline, etc.) will cause the check to fail.
name: task_000000_5838556c
